examName: AWS AIF-C01 Mock Exam - Advanced Depth

questions.text: A company is building a generative AI application using Amazon Bedrock. The security team requires that all model invocation logs be retained for 7 years for compliance with financial regulations. The logs must include which IAM user or role made each request. Which combination of services should the company use?
options: Amazon CloudWatch Logs with export to S3, plus AWS Config for compliance monitoring
AWS CloudTrail with log file validation enabled, plus S3 lifecycle policies for retention
Amazon GuardDuty with S3 export, plus IAM access analyzer for user attribution
AWS Trusted Advisor with CloudWatch dashboards, plus S3 Glacier for archival
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records Bedrock API calls including user/role identity. Log file validation ensures integrity. S3 lifecycle policies can enforce 7-year retention by moving to Glacier after a period. CloudWatch Logs doesn't natively capture API caller identity at this level of detail for Bedrock.

questions.text: A media company wants to build a semantic search engine over their 500,000-hour video library. Users should be able to search for phrases like "the scene where the protagonist argues with his father about the inheritance." Which AWS architecture is MOST appropriate for this use case?
options: Amazon Transcribe to convert audio to text, then Amazon Kendra for semantic search over transcripts
Amazon Rekognition Video to extract objects and faces, then OpenSearch for metadata search
Amazon Bedrock with Claude to generate video descriptions, then RDS with pgvector for similarity search
Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth to manually label every scene, then S3 Select for text search
questions.explanation: Transcribe converts speech to searchable text. Kendra provides intelligent semantic search with natural language understanding. Option B lacks transcript content. Option C would be extremely expensive and slower. Option D is not scalable for 500k hours.

questions.text: A developer is using Amazon Bedrock with a foundation model for a customer service chatbot. They notice that when a customer asks the same question in slightly different ways, the model sometimes gives contradictory answers. The developer wants to make responses more consistent without retraining the model. Which approach would be MOST effective?
options: Lower the temperature parameter to make outputs more deterministic
Increase the temperature parameter to reduce randomness
Fine-tune the model on customer service transcripts
Implement a RAG knowledge base with customer service policies
questions.explanation: Lowering temperature makes model outputs more deterministic and consistent (temperature 0 = always same response for same input). Increasing temperature increases randomness/creativity. Fine-tuning requires training data and is overkill for this specific issue. RAG adds context but doesn't directly control consistency.

questions.text: A bank deploys an AI model to screen loan applications. After six months, an audit reveals the model rejects applicants from a certain postal code at twice the rate of other areas. Investigation shows the postal code has a high concentration of minority residents, but the model was not explicitly trained on postal code as a feature. What is the MOST likely cause of this disparate impact?
options: The model is overfitting to the training data
The model is using correlated proxy features that correlate with postal code
The model has high variance in its predictions
The model needs more training data to generalize better
questions.explanation: Disparate impact often occurs through proxy features — variables that correlate with protected attributes (e.g., zip code correlating with race/income). The model may have learned patterns from correlated features even without explicit protected attributes. Overfitting and variance describe different problems, not systematic bias.

questions.text: A data scientist is evaluating two churn prediction models. Model A has 95% accuracy, 85% precision, and 60% recall. Model B has 93% accuracy, 75% precision, and 90% recall. The business impact of a false negative (failing to identify a customer who will churn) is losing $500 in revenue. The cost of a false positive (offering a discount to a customer who would not have churned) is $50. Which model should the company choose and why?
options: Model A because it has higher precision, reducing false positives and saving $50 per incident
Model B because it has higher recall, capturing more actual churn cases despite more false positives
Model A because it has higher accuracy overall, minimizing total errors
Model B because it has higher precision, which is more important for customer retention
questions.explanation: False negative cost ($500) is 10x higher than false positive ($50). Model B's higher recall (90% vs 60%) means capturing 30% more actual churn cases, saving significantly more despite increased false positives. Accuracy is misleading here due to class imbalance considerations. Precision alone doesn't address the asymmetric cost.

questions.text: A healthcare company wants to build a system that extracts patient information from scanned medical forms, identifies medical conditions and medications mentioned, and then answers doctor questions about the patient's history. Which AWS service combination is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon Textract → Amazon Comprehend Medical → Amazon Bedrock with RAG
Amazon Rekognition → Amazon Comprehend → Amazon Lex
Amazon Transcribe → Amazon HealthLake → Amazon Polly
Amazon Textract → Amazon Translate → Amazon Kendra
questions.explanation: Textract extracts text from scanned forms (including handwriting). Comprehend Medical identifies medical entities (conditions, medications). Bedrock with RAG enables Q&A on the extracted information. HealthLake is for storing healthcare data but not for extraction from forms. Translate is for language translation, not relevant here.

questions.text: An e-commerce company uses Amazon Bedrock to generate product descriptions. The compliance team requires that the model never generate descriptions that mention competitor products or make unsubstantiated health claims. The company has identified 50 specific phrases that should never appear. Which approach should they take?
options: Use Bedrock Guardrails with a denied topics list for competitor products and health claims
Fine-tune the model on approved product descriptions only
Use prompt engineering with negative prompts to explicitly forbid the 50 phrases
Implement a post-processing Lambda function to filter generated descriptions
Use Bedrock Agents to override any prohibited content
questions.explanation: Guardrails provide native content filtering and denied topics (best for broad categories like "competitor products"). Post-processing Lambda provides additional filtering for specific phrases (the 50 exact matches). Fine-tuning doesn't guarantee prevention of certain phrases. Prompt engineering alone isn't reliable for blocking. Agents are for actions, not filtering.

questions.text: Which of the following accurately describes the difference between MLOps and DevOps? (Select TWO)
options: MLOps must handle data versioning and model versioning, while DevOps primarily handles code versioning
MLOps requires monitoring for data drift and model drift, which DevOps does not typically address
DevOps uses CI/CD pipelines while MLOps does not require any automation
MLOps eliminates the need for testing because models are probabilistic
DevOps cannot be applied to machine learning workloads because they require specialized infrastructure
questions.explanation: MLOps extends DevOps to handle data versioning, model versioning, and unique ML concerns like data drift and model drift. Both use CI/CD automation. ML still requires testing. DevOps principles apply to ML, but specialized tooling is needed.

questions.text: A company wants to build a customer support chatbot that can answer questions from their product documentation, user manuals, and FAQ pages. The documentation is updated daily. The chatbot must always provide answers based on the latest documentation without requiring model retraining. Which architecture should they choose?
options: Fine-tune a foundation model on all documentation daily using SageMaker
Use Bedrock Knowledge Bases with a scheduled ingestion job to sync updated documents
Build a custom RAG pipeline using OpenSearch with a Lambda function for daily re-indexing
Use prompt engineering with daily updated context injected into each prompt
questions.explanation: Bedrock Knowledge Bases natively support scheduled ingestion to keep vector stores current without retraining. Option 2 could work but requires custom development. Option 3 would be limited by prompt token limits and not scale to full documentation. Option 1 requires daily fine-tuning which is expensive and unnecessary.

questions.text: A company is deploying a generative AI model to summarize customer support tickets. Before deployment, they test the model on 10,000 historical tickets. The model accurately summarizes 98% of tickets but occasionally adds details that were not present in the original ticket. What is the appropriate remediation strategy?
options: Lower the temperature parameter to reduce hallucination
Implement a RAG system to ground responses in source documents
Fine-tune the model with a penalty for hallucinated content
Add a human reviewer for all summaries
questions.explanation: Lowering temperature reduces randomness and creativity, which helps prevent hallucinations (generating details not in source). RAG helps for Q&A but summarization already has source content. Fine-tuning might help but is more resource-intensive. Human review for all summaries defeats automation benefits and is overkill for 2% error rate.

questions.text: Match each inference parameter to its effect on foundation model output:
rightItems: Controls randomness/creativity of output
Controls nucleus sampling diversity
Limits maximum response length
Specifies where to cut off generation
questions.explanation: Temperature controls randomness; Top P controls nucleus sampling; Max Tokens limits length; Stop Sequences defines endpoints.

questions.text: Place the following steps for implementing least privilege access to Amazon Bedrock foundation models in the correct order:
options: Create IAM policy allowing specific model ARNs
Attach IAM role to the application
Identify which foundation models the application needs
Create IAM role with trust policy for the application service
Test application access with minimal permissions
questions.explanation: First identify required models, then create policy allowing those specific ARNs, create role, attach to application, then test.

questions.text: A software company wants to use Amazon Q Developer to improve developer productivity. Which statement about Amazon Q Developer's capabilities is accurate?
options: Amazon Q Developer can generate code, explain existing code, and suggest fixes within IDEs
Amazon Q Developer can only answer questions about AWS services, not generate code
Amazon Q Developer requires all source code to be stored in AWS CodeCommit
Amazon Q Developer only works with Python and JavaScript
questions.explanation: Amazon Q Developer provides code generation, explanation, and debugging across multiple languages and IDEs. It does not require CodeCommit and works with many languages.

questions.text: A company has 1 million customer support chat logs. They want to identify common topics and themes without pre-labeled categories. Which approach should they use?
options: Supervised learning with manual labeling of 100,000 examples
Topic modeling using unsupervised learning (e.g., Latent Dirichlet Allocation)
Reinforcement learning with reward for correct topic identification
Regression analysis to predict topic probabilities
questions.explanation: Topic modeling is unsupervised learning that discovers themes without labels. Supervised requires labeled data. RL is for sequential decisions. Regression predicts continuous values, not topics.

questions.text: Match each responsible AI concept to its definition:
rightItems: Model does not discriminate against protected groups
Ability to understand why a model made a decision
Model performs reliably under changing conditions
Open documentation of model capabilities and limitations
questions.explanation: Fairness prevents discrimination. Explainability enables understanding. Robustness ensures reliability. Transparency requires open disclosure.

questions.text: A developer is building a prompt for a foundation model to extract structured data from customer emails. The prompt includes: 'Extract the customer name (field: name), order number (field: order_id), and issue type (field: issue) from the following email. Return ONLY a JSON object with these fields.' This is an example of:
options: Zero-shot prompting with structured output instructions
Few-shot prompting with examples
Chain-of-thought prompting
Role-based prompting
questions.explanation: This is zero-shot because no examples are provided — just instructions on what to extract and the output format.

questions.text: A manufacturing company has sensor data from 1,000 machines. They want to detect when a machine is operating abnormally, but they have no labeled examples of "abnormal" operation. Which approach should they use?
options: Anomaly detection using unsupervised learning
Classification using supervised learning
Regression using labeled normal data
Reinforcement learning with anomaly reward
questions.explanation: Anomaly detection is unsupervised — it identifies patterns that deviate from normal without requiring labeled anomalies. Supervised needs labeled abnormal data.

questions.text: Which AWS services are MOST appropriate for detecting and automatically remediating non-compliant resource configurations? (Select TWO)
options: AWS Config with SSM Automation documents
Amazon Inspector
AWS CloudTrail
AWS Config rules with automatic remediation
Amazon GuardDuty
questions.explanation: AWS Config can evaluate resources against rules and trigger automatic remediation via SSM Automation. Config rules can also have built-in remediation actions. Inspector scans vulnerabilities but doesn't remediate. CloudTrail logs activity. GuardDuty detects threats.

questions.text: A company is using Amazon Bedrock with a foundation model. They want to implement a multi-step workflow where the model checks inventory, updates a database, and schedules shipments. Which Bedrock feature should they use?
options: Bedrock Knowledge Bases
Bedrock Guardrails
Bedrock Agents
Bedrock Model Evaluation
questions.explanation: Bedrock Agents handle multi-step tasks and integrate with APIs/systems (inventory, database, shipping). Knowledge Bases are for RAG. Guardrails for safety. Model Evaluation for comparing models.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of Amazon SageMaker Clarify?
options: To monitor deployed models for data drift
To detect bias in training data and model predictions, and provide feature importance explanations
To debug training jobs by monitoring gradients and activations
To track and compare training experiments
questions.explanation: SageMaker Clarify is specifically for bias detection and model explainability (feature importance). Model Monitor detects drift. Debugger debugs training. Experiments tracks runs.

questions.text: A company is deploying an AI model that recommends financial products to customers. Regulatory requirements mandate that customers receive an explanation of why they were recommended a specific product. Which capability addresses this requirement?
options: Model fairness metrics
Model explainability (SHAP values, feature importance)
Model robustness testing
Model privacy preservation
questions.explanation: Explainability techniques like SHAP values provide feature-level explanations for individual predictions, enabling customer-facing explanations.

questions.text: Match each foundation model customization approach to its cost and effort level:
rightItems: Highest cost, requires massive data
Moderate cost, requires domain-specific data
Lowest cost, no training data needed
Moderate cost, requires vector database
questions.explanation: Pre-training is most expensive. Fine-tuning needs domain data. In-context learning uses examples in prompt. RAG requires vector infrastructure.

questions.text: A company uses Amazon Bedrock to process customer support inquiries containing PII. The security team requires that no PII is stored in model logs. Which approach should they take?
options: Use Amazon Comprehend to redact PII from logs after they are written
Configure Bedrock Guardrails with content filtering to block PII
Use AWS Lambda to encrypt logs before writing to CloudWatch
Disable logging for all Bedrock API calls
questions.explanation: Guardrails can detect and block PII from being generated or logged. Post-processing redaction would still store PII temporarily. Disabling logs prevents security auditing — not compliant with most regulations.

questions.text: Which of the following are limitations of generative AI models that must be considered when designing applications? (Select TWO)
options: Hallucinations — generating plausible but factually incorrect information
Nondeterminism — same input may produce different outputs
Perfect accuracy — models never make mistakes
Deterministic outputs — always same response for same input
Inability to process natural language
questions.explanation: Hallucinations and nondeterminism are key limitations. Models are not perfectly accurate. Outputs are nondeterministic (especially with higher temperature). Models can process natural language.

questions.text: A developer is comparing two foundation models in Amazon Bedrock. Model A costs $0.01 per 1,000 input tokens and has 5 second average latency. Model B costs $0.05 per 1,000 input tokens and has 1 second average latency. The application requires real-time responses for 1 million requests per day with average input length of 500 tokens. Which model is MORE cost-effective and meets the latency requirement?
options: Model A is more cost-effective at $5,000/day but may not meet latency needs
Model B is more cost-effective overall despite higher per-token cost
Model A is cheaper at $5,000/day vs $25,000/day for Model B, so choose Model A
Neither model meets the latency requirement for real-time responses
questions.explanation: Model A: $0.01 × (1M × 500/1000) = $5,000/day. Model B: $0.05 × (1M × 500/1000) = $25,000/day. Model A is 5x cheaper. Latency 5 seconds may be too slow for real-time. Question tests evaluating tradeoffs between cost and performance.

questions.text: Which statement accurately describes the difference between batch inference and real-time inference?
options: Batch inference processes multiple requests together and is typically cheaper; real-time inference processes single requests with lower latency
Batch inference provides lower latency than real-time inference
Real-time inference processes multiple requests together and is cheaper
There is no difference; they are interchangeable terms
questions.explanation: Batch inference processes groups of requests asynchronously — cheaper but higher latency. Real-time inference processes single requests synchronously — higher cost but lower latency.

questions.text: A company is building a resume screening AI. They want to ensure the model does not discriminate based on gender or ethnicity. Which approaches should they implement? (Select TWO)
options: Remove demographic fields from training data and inputs
Test model for disparate impact using statistical parity metrics
Add demographic fields to training data to explicitly account for them
Use only names as features since names indicate gender
Train only on data from a single demographic to reduce variance
questions.explanation: Remove demographic fields to prevent direct discrimination. Test for disparate impact using metrics like statistical parity. Adding demographic fields or using names (gender proxies) would likely introduce bias. Training on single demographic would harm generalization.

questions.text: Place the following steps for auditing Bedrock API activity for security compliance in the correct order:
options: Query CloudTrail logs for Bedrock events
Identify suspicious patterns (e.g., unusual model invocation volume)
Enable CloudTrail for all AWS regions
Set up S3 bucket for CloudTrail log storage
Create CloudWatch alarm for anomaly detection
questions.explanation: First enable CloudTrail, create storage bucket, then query logs, identify suspicious patterns, and set up automated alerts.

questions.text: A company wants to build a document processing pipeline that extracts information from loan applications, identifies inconsistencies, and flags applications for human review. Which AWS service is MOST appropriate for the human review workflow?
options: Amazon Mechanical Turk
Amazon Augmented AI (A2I)
Amazon Rekognition
Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
questions.explanation: A2I is specifically for adding human review to AI predictions. Mechanical Turk is for crowdsourcing simple tasks. Ground Truth is for creating labeled datasets, not production review workflows.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the 'temperature' parameter in foundation model inference?
options: Controls the physical temperature of the GPU to optimize performance
Controls the randomness and creativity of token selection
Sets the maximum number of tokens in the response
Defines the context window size for the model
questions.explanation: Temperature controls randomness — lower = more deterministic/predictable, higher = more creative/diverse outputs.

questions.text: Match each ML metric to its definition:
rightItems: TP/(TP+FP) — correctness of positive predictions
TP/(TP+FN) — completeness of positive predictions
Harmonic mean of precision and recall
(TP+TN)/(TP+TN+FP+FN) — overall correctness
questions.explanation: Precision = TP/(TP+FP). Recall = TP/(TP+FN). F1 balances both. Accuracy = (TP+TN)/total.

questions.text: A developer is using Amazon Bedrock with a prompt: 'You are a customer service agent for an airline. A passenger asks: "My flight was delayed and I missed my connection. What are my options?" Respond as the agent.' This is an example of which prompt engineering technique?
options: Zero-shot prompting
Few-shot prompting
Chain-of-thought prompting
Role-based prompting
questions.explanation: Role-based prompting assigns a persona to the model ('You are a customer service agent').

questions.text: A company deploys a generative AI model to summarize news articles. After deployment, they discover the model produces different summaries for articles about different demographic groups. What is the appropriate investigation step?
options: Immediately retrain the model on more diverse data
Analyze training data for representation bias across demographic groups
Increase the temperature parameter to add randomness
Disable the model and return to manual summarization
questions.explanation: First investigate training data bias — likely cause is underrepresentation of certain groups. Retraining without analysis may not address root cause.

questions.text: Which AWS service would you use to receive alerts when an IAM policy is modified to allow public access to S3 buckets containing sensitive data?
options: AWS Config with S3.ALLOW_PUBLIC_READ rule and SNS notification
Amazon GuardDuty with S3 protection enabled
AWS CloudTrail with CloudWatch Events
Amazon Inspector with S3 assessment
questions.explanation: AWS Config can evaluate S3 bucket policies against rules and trigger SNS alerts. GuardDuty detects threats but not policy changes. CloudTrail logs changes but doesn't evaluate compliance. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities.

questions.text: A model trained to classify product reviews as positive or negative achieves 99% accuracy on the test set. However, the test set has 98% positive reviews and 2% negative. The model predicted 'positive' for every review. What is the model's recall for the negative class?
questions.explanation: Recall for negative class = TP_negative/(TP_negative+FN_negative). Model predicted zero negative reviews, so TP_negative = 0, recall = 0%.

questions.text: A company wants to build a system that automatically generates alt text for images on their website to improve accessibility. Which AWS service combination should they use?
options: Amazon Rekognition to detect objects and scenes, then Amazon Bedrock to generate descriptive text
Amazon Textract to extract text from images, then Amazon Polly to convert to speech
Amazon Transcribe to process image metadata, then Amazon Comprehend for sentiment
Amazon Kendra to search for similar images, then Amazon Lex to describe them
questions.explanation: Rekognition detects image content (objects, scenes, actions). Bedrock generates natural language descriptions from those detections.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF)?
options: To reduce model training time using human-labeled data
To align model outputs with human preferences and values
To increase model size without additional computing resources
To translate model outputs into multiple languages
questions.explanation: RLHF uses human preference data to fine-tune models, aligning outputs with human values and desired behaviors.

questions.text: Which AWS services are MOST appropriate for discovering and protecting sensitive data in data lakes? (Select TWO)
options: Amazon Macie
AWS Config
Amazon GuardDuty
Amazon Inspector
AWS Lake Formation
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie discovers and classifies sensitive data using ML. Lake Formation provides fine-grained access controls for data lakes. Config tracks configuration. GuardDuty detects threats. Inspector scans vulnerabilities.

questions.text: Match each prompt engineering risk to its description:
rightItems: Malicious input that hijacks model behavior
Model reveals its system prompt or training data
Bypassing safety guardrails and content filters
Model generates factually incorrect information
questions.explanation: Injection hijacks behavior; leaking reveals prompts; jailbreaking bypasses safety; hallucination generates false info.

questions.text: A developer is evaluating a foundation model for a translation application. They test the model on 1,000 sentences and achieve a BLEU score of 0.85. What does this indicate?
options: The model has 85% accuracy in translation tasks
The model's translations are very similar to human reference translations
The model is hallucinating 15% of its outputs
The model has high precision but low recall
questions.explanation: BLEU (Bilingual Evaluation Understudy) measures similarity between machine translation and human reference translations. Higher scores (0-1) indicate better quality.

questions.text: Which AWS service is designed for real-time personalization and product recommendations?
options: Amazon Forecast
Amazon Personalize
Amazon SageMaker
Amazon Kendra
questions.explanation: Amazon Personalize is purpose-built for real-time recommendations. Forecast is for time-series. SageMaker for custom models. Kendra for search.

questions.text: A company wants to implement human review for low-confidence predictions from their document classification model. Which service should they use?
options: Amazon A2I (Augmented AI)
Amazon Mechanical Turk
SageMaker Ground Truth
Amazon Rekognition
questions.explanation: A2I is specifically for adding human review to AI predictions. Mechanical Turk is for simple crowdsourcing tasks. Ground Truth for labeling datasets.

questions.text: Place the following steps for securing a Bedrock-powered application with IAM in the correct order:
options: Create IAM role with least privilege permissions
Attach IAM role to application service
Identify required Bedrock actions (InvokeModel, etc.)
Create IAM policy specifying allowed model ARNs
Test application access
questions.explanation: First identify required actions, create policy specifying allowed models, create role, attach to application, then test.

questions.text: A company wants to build a chatbot that can answer questions about their internal policies stored in Confluence, SharePoint, and S3. The chatbot must provide citations showing which document the answer came from. Which architecture should they use?
options: Fine-tune a foundation model on all documents and rely on model memory
Use Bedrock Knowledge Bases with multiple data source connectors and source attribution
Build a custom RAG pipeline with OpenSearch and manually add citations
Use Amazon Q Business with its built-in connector framework
questions.explanation: Amazon Q Business natively connects to Confluence, SharePoint, and S3, and provides source attribution. Bedrock Knowledge Bases also works but requires more custom setup.

questions.text: Which statement correctly describes a key difference between traditional programming and machine learning?
options: Traditional programming uses explicit rules; ML learns rules from data
ML does not require any data to function
Traditional programming can only work with structured data
ML produces deterministic outputs every time
questions.explanation: Traditional programming: rules + data → answers. ML: answers + data → rules.

questions.text: Match each responsible AI practice to its purpose:
rightItems: Identifying unfair model behavior
Reducing representation gaps
Adding human oversight for critical decisions
Documenting model capabilities and limitations
questions.explanation: Bias detection identifies unfairness. Diverse data reduces gaps. Human-in-the-loop adds oversight. Model cards document.

questions.text: A developer is using few-shot prompting with a foundation model. They provide 3 examples of input-output pairs, then ask for a new input. What is the primary benefit of this approach over zero-shot?
options: Lower cost because fewer tokens are processed
Better accuracy on tasks with specific output formats or patterns
Faster response times with fewer examples
Eliminates all hallucinations from the model
questions.explanation: Few-shot examples help the model understand desired format and patterns, improving accuracy on specific tasks. It actually costs more tokens. Doesn't eliminate hallucinations.

questions.text: What is the purpose of Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor?
options: To track training experiments and compare hyperparameters
To detect data drift and model drift after deployment
To debug training jobs and identify gradient issues
To detect bias in training data and model predictions
questions.explanation: Model Monitor specifically monitors deployed models for data drift, model drift, and data quality issues.

questions.text: A company uses multiple AWS accounts for development, testing, and production. They want to centrally audit all Bedrock API calls across all accounts. Which AWS service should they use?
options: AWS Organizations with CloudTrail organization trail
Amazon GuardDuty with multi-account protection
AWS Config with aggregator
Amazon Inspector with multi-account assessment
questions.explanation: AWS Organizations enables CloudTrail organization trails that log API activity across all member accounts to a central S3 bucket.

questions.text: A developer notices that their foundation model consistently produces poor results when asked to reason about multi-step problems. Which prompt engineering technique would MOST directly address this issue?
options: Chain-of-thought prompting with step-by-step reasoning
Few-shot prompting with more examples
Role-based prompting as a logical thinker
Negative prompts to avoid common errors
questions.explanation: Chain-of-thought explicitly asks the model to break down reasoning into steps, improving performance on multi-step problems.

questions.text: Which of the following accurately describe Amazon Bedrock's capabilities? (Select TWO)
options: Provides access to foundation models from multiple providers (Anthropic, Meta, Amazon)
Requires all models to be fine-tuned before use
Includes Guardrails for content filtering and denied topics
Only works with text-based models, not multimodal
Cannot be integrated with Lambda for business logic
questions.explanation: Bedrock offers multiple provider models (Claude, Llama, Titan). Guardrails provide safety features. Models work fine without fine-tuning. Bedrock supports multimodal (Titan Image, etc.). Lambda integration is supported.

questions.text: A company is concerned about prompt injection attacks on their generative AI customer service chatbot. Which mitigation strategy is MOST effective?
options: Increase temperature to make outputs less predictable
Implement input validation and use Bedrock Guardrails
Reduce the context window to limit prompt length
Fine-tune the model to ignore all user input
questions.explanation: Input validation (sanitizing user input) and Guardrails provide defense against prompt injection. Increasing temperature doesn't help. Reducing context window might break functionality. Fine-tuning to ignore input defeats purpose.

questions.text: A data scientist is building a model to predict maintenance needs for industrial equipment. They have sensor data (temperature, vibration, pressure) and labeled examples of past failures. What type of ML problem is this?
options: Unsupervised learning (clustering)
Supervised learning (classification or regression)
Reinforcement learning
Generative AI
questions.explanation: With labeled examples of past failures, this is supervised learning. It could be classification (will fail vs not) or regression (time until failure).

questions.text: A company wants to generate personalized email marketing content for 1 million customers based on their purchase history. The content must be unique per customer and generated in real-time. Which approach is MOST scalable?
options: Generate all 1 million emails offline in a batch job
Use Amazon Bedrock with Lambda to generate emails on-demand
Write 1,000 templates and manually select for each customer
Train a custom model to output all emails simultaneously
questions.explanation: On-demand generation with Bedrock and Lambda scales automatically. Batch generation would have high latency. Manual templates aren't truly personalized. Custom model training is resource-intensive.

questions.text: Which AWS service would you use to assess your AWS configuration against security best practices and receive recommendations for improvement?
options: AWS Trusted Advisor
Amazon Inspector
AWS Config
AWS Security Hub
questions.explanation: Trusted Advisor provides best practice recommendations for security, cost, performance, and fault tolerance.

questions.text: Place the stages of the ML development lifecycle in the correct order:
options: Model Deployment
Model Training
Business Problem Definition
Data Collection & Preparation
Model Evaluation
questions.explanation: First define business problem, collect data, train model, evaluate, then deploy.

questions.text: A developer is using Amazon Bedrock with a foundation model for a code generation task. They want the model to generate Python code that follows specific company style guidelines. Which approach would be MOST effective?
options: Fine-tune the model on company code with style guidelines
Use few-shot prompting with examples following style guidelines
Use zero-shot prompting with instructions about style
Implement post-processing to reformat code
questions.explanation: Few-shot prompting with examples showing desired style is often most effective for code generation tasks. Fine-tuning is overkill for style rules. Post-processing only reformats, doesn't fix generation.

questions.text: What is the purpose of model cards in responsible AI?
options: To physically store model weights
To document model capabilities, limitations, and intended use cases
To track API usage and billing
To encrypt model predictions
questions.explanation: Model cards document transparency information: what the model does, its limitations, training data, evaluation metrics, and intended use cases.

questions.text: Match each foundation model concept to its description:
rightItems: Basic unit of text processing (word fragment)
Numerical vector representation of text
Maximum input length the model can process
Allows model to focus on relevant parts of input
questions.explanation: Tokens are text fragments. Embeddings are numerical representations. Context window is max length. Attention focuses on relevant input.

questions.text: Which AWS service would you use to automatically discover and classify sensitive data (like credit card numbers) in S3 buckets?
options: Amazon Inspector
AWS Config
Amazon Macie
Amazon GuardDuty
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to discover and classify sensitive data in S3. Inspector scans for vulnerabilities. Config tracks configurations. GuardDuty detects threats.

questions.text: A company wants to build a system that automatically summarizes customer service call recordings. The system must handle calls in multiple languages. Which service combination should they use?
options: Amazon Transcribe (with language identification) → Amazon Translate → Amazon Bedrock (summarization)
Amazon Polly → Amazon Comprehend → Amazon Lex
Amazon Rekognition → Amazon Textract → Amazon Kendra
Amazon Transcribe → Amazon Polly → Amazon Personalize
questions.explanation: Transcribe converts speech to text with language ID. Translate converts to target language if needed. Bedrock generates summary.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of unsupervised learning?
options: Predicting house prices from square footage
Grouping customers by purchase patterns without labels
Detecting spam emails from labeled examples
Playing chess through reward signals
questions.explanation: Customer grouping without labels is clustering — unsupervised. House prices and spam detection are supervised. Chess is RL.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of Amazon SageMaker Clarify?
options: To monitor deployed models for data drift
To detect bias in training data and model predictions
To track training experiments and hyperparameters
To debug training jobs and identify issues
questions.explanation: Clarify is specifically for bias detection and explainability. Model Monitor is for drift. Experiments for tracking. Debugger for debugging.

questions.text: A developer is building a prompt for a foundation model to extract structured data from emails. Which prompt design is MOST likely to produce consistent, parseable output?
options: "Extract the important information from this email."
"Extract the customer name, order number, and issue. Return ONLY a valid JSON object with fields: name, order_id, issue."
"What is the customer's name, order number, and what is the issue?"
"This email contains customer information. Please analyze it thoroughly."
questions.explanation: Specific instructions with output format constraints (ONLY JSON) produce the most consistent parseable output.

questions.text: Which statement accurately describes the AWS Shared Responsibility Model for AI services like Amazon Bedrock?
options: AWS is responsible for securing the underlying infrastructure; customer is responsible for data, prompts, and access management
AWS is responsible for all security aspects of AI services
Customer is responsible for all security aspects of AI services
Security is automatically handled by the AI model itself
questions.explanation: AWS secures the infrastructure (hardware, software, networking). Customers secure their data, prompts, IAM configurations, and application-level security.
